title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes makes use of a number of certificates as part of its operation. You should set the ownership of the directory containing the PKI information and all files in that directory to maintain their integrity. The directory and files should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, ls -laR /etc/kubernetes/pki/ Verify that the ownership of all files and directories in this hierarchy is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chown -R root:root /etc/kubernetes/pki/
input_row.default_value: By default, the /etc/kubernetes/pki/ directory and all of the files and directories contained within it, are set to be owned by the root user.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Control Plane node where Kubernetes is installed.
2. Run the command: `ls -laR /etc/kubernetes/pki/`
3. Verify that the ownership of all files and directories in this hierarchy is set to `root:root`.
4. Confirm that all files and directories are owned by the root user and the root group, ensuring the integrity of the PKI information. 

Expected secure configuration: All files and directories under `/etc/kubernetes/pki/` should have the ownership set to `root:root`. 

To confirm compliance, ensure that the output of the `ls -laR` command shows `root root` as the owner for all items listed.
